channel.title: Delicious posts Feed - Pinterest
channel.description: Fresh posts and cooking inspiration for Pinterest discovery

item.title: Classic Banana Bread That Feels Like Home in Every Slice
item.description: This banana bread is my go-to comfort bake — moist, sweet, and packed with ripe banana flavor. I use brown sugar for a subtle caramel touch. Perfect for breakfast or an afternoon snack with tea. | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 60min | Serves: 8 | #12 #Easypost #post #cooking #foodie
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 60 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 75 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 3/4 cups (220g) all-purpose flour, sifted
  • 1 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 cup (115g) unsalted butter, softened
  • 1 cup (200g) packed brown sugar
  • 2 large eggs, room temperature
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 3 ripe bananas, mashed (about 1 1/2 cups)
  • 1/4 cup (60ml) buttermilk or plain yogurt

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x5 inch loaf pan well, or line it with parchment paper. This ensures your bread won’t stick and will come out cleanly. Properly preparing the pan is key to keeping the loaf intact after baking. If you skip this, you might end up with a crumbly mess or lose some of the crust that makes banana bread so delicious. 10 minutes Pan is evenly greased or parchment is fully covering the bottom and sides
  2. 2 In a medium bowl, whisk together the sifted flour, baking soda, and salt. This dry mix is the backbone of your bread’s structure. Sifting helps remove lumps and aerates the flour, resulting in a lighter crumb. Baking soda reacts with the acidity in the bananas and buttermilk to help the bread rise nicely. Don’t skip this step or your loaf might be dense. 5 minutes Dry ingredients are fully combined and free of lumps
  3. 3 In a large bowl, cream the softened butter and brown sugar together until light and fluffy using a hand mixer or stand mixer. This step incorporates air into the fat, which helps create a tender texture in the bread. If you just mix them quickly, your bread might turn out dense or heavy. Aim for a pale, creamy mixture before moving on. 3-5 minutes Butter and sugar look lighter in color and have a fluffy texture
  4. 4 Add the eggs one at a time to the butter mixture, beating well after each addition. This ensures they’re fully incorporated and helps maintain the batter’s smooth texture. If you add both eggs at once, the mixture might curdle or become uneven, affecting how the bread bakes. 3 minutes Mixture is smooth and homogenous with no visible egg streaks
  5. 5 Mix in the vanilla extract and mashed bananas until just combined. Overmixing here can lead to a tough loaf since the bananas add moisture and sugar. The bananas should be very ripe and mashed well to distribute flavor evenly. This step adds the signature banana flavor and moisture that makes this bread so special. 2 minutes Batter has a uniform banana color with no large chunks
  6. 6 Alternately add the dry ingredients and buttermilk to the banana mixture in three parts, starting and ending with the dry ingredients. Gently fold each addition with a spatula until just combined. This alternating method keeps the batter light and prevents overworking the gluten, which would make the bread dense. If you rush or skip folding, the texture can suffer. 5 minutes Batter is smooth with no flour pockets but still slightly lumpy from bananas
  7. 7 Pour the batter into your prepared loaf pan, smoothing the top with a spatula. This ensures even baking and a nice shape. Tap the pan lightly on the counter to release any air bubbles trapped inside. Air bubbles can cause holes or uneven texture in the final bread. 3 minutes Batter is evenly distributed and surface is smooth
  8. 8 Bake in the preheated oven for 55-65 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean or with just a few moist crumbs. Avoid opening the oven too often during baking, or the bread might sink in the middle. If the top browns too quickly, tent loosely with foil halfway through baking. 55-65 minutes Golden brown top and toothpick test passes
  9. 9 Let the bread cool in the pan for about 15 minutes before transferring it to a wire rack to cool completely. Cooling in the pan allows the bread to set so it doesn’t crumble when you remove it. Patience here is key — slicing too soon can make it fall apart. Once cooled, you can slice and enjoy or store it for later. 15-20 minutes Bread feels firm to the touch and easily releases from the pan

📌 Pinterest Tags: #12 #Easypost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #allpurpose #teaspoon

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:description: This banana bread is my go-to comfort bake — moist, sweet, and packed with ripe banana flavor. I use brown sugar for a subtle caramel touch. Perfect for breakfast or an afternoon snack with tea.
